mnemonics.title: Types of Input Devices
mnemonics.content: P-S-S-A-B: Pointing, Scanning, Sound, Audio, Biometric - helps recall main categories

acronyms.usage: Converts scanned images of text into editable digital text
Detects marked bubbles or checkboxes on forms for automated processing
Reads magnetically charged characters on bank checks
Light sensor technology used in scanners and digital cameras
Applications using light pens and graphics tablets for design work
Standard protocol for connecting musical instruments as input devices

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a laser beam dancing across black-and-white zebra stripes, translating light-dark patterns into computer code like a translator reading a foreign language
Picture thousands of tiny camera flashes going off under your mouse, capturing surface snapshots faster than you can blink, comparing them like flipbook animation
Think of a slow-moving flashlight scan across a photograph, with thousands of light-sensitive eyes reading each tiny point and painting a digital picture
Imagine your body's unique features (fingerprint ridges, iris patterns) as a personal password that never changes and can't be forgotten

analogies.analogy: Like a piano keyboard where each key produces a specific note, each keyboard key produces a specific character code for the computer to understand
Like your hand reaching out to point at or grab things on your desk, the mouse reaches into the screen to point at or select items
Like a photocopier that creates a digital copy instead of paper - it's like taking a photograph and saving it as a computer file
Like touching items on a physical display board directly, instead of using a remote stick to point - it's the most natural, direct interaction
Like a digital drawing board where your pen strokes appear instantly on screen, similar to an artist tracing on transparent paper over a sketch
